_meta.source: The Library Canonical Philosophy — Chart Bridge Mapping
_meta.description: Maps each Library overtone to specific Human Design chart structures, enabling the synthesis to explain WHY chart elements behave as they do at a structural level.
_meta.usage: Injected into RAG context. For each chart element, look up the relevant overtone to provide structural explanation.

polarity.name: Polarity
polarity.structuralPrinciple: Two mutually defining, mutually requiring aspects that cannot exist independently and cannot collapse into identity.

hdApplication.primary: Every defined center creates its polarity partner in the undefined centers. A defined Sacral (consistent life force) creates the polarity of an undefined Sacral (amplified, inconsistent life force). Neither state is complete without the other.
hdApplication.centerPairDynamics: The defined center holds consistent charge; the undefined center receives and amplifies. The membrane between them (P5 Threshold) is the most dynamically active zone — both most susceptible to conditioning AND most productive for relational exchange.
hdApplication.practicalInsight: When explaining a defined/undefined center pair, name BOTH poles. The defined center is not 'better' — it is one pole of a charge relationship. The person's distinctive quality lives in the threshold between what they hold consistently and what they absorb variably.

expressions.P1_Wave: Any continuous process in the chart alternates between two states. Emotional waves, sacral on/off, splenic alerts — all are polarity dynamics.
expressions.P2_Charge: What consistent charge does a defined center hold? What charge does the undefined center receive and amplify from the environment?
expressions.P3_Phase: Wave polarity extended across time — constructive interference (aligned energy) and destructive interference (conflicting energy) between chart activations.
expressions.P4_Field: Every defined center generates a field that structures relational space. A defined Solar Plexus generates an emotional field others enter and respond to.
expressions.P5_Threshold: The membrane between defined and undefined — where conditioning meets consistency. The most dynamically active zone in the chart.

trinity.name: Trinity
trinity.structuralPrinciple: Three terms whose mutual interaction sustains activity that two terms cannot sustain, generating emergence that neither term possesses alone.

hdApplication.primary: Type, Strategy, and Authority form a three-body system. Any two-body subset is unstable: Type without Authority is directionless; Authority without Strategy is ungrounded; Strategy without Type has no vessel. Only the three-way interaction produces the person's specific emergent behavioral pattern.
hdApplication.nameTheThird: The Third is the emergent behavior that neither Strategy nor Authority predicts alone. For a Generator with Emotional Authority: the Third is not 'respond' (Strategy) or 'wait for clarity' (Authority) — it is the specific quality of embodied patience that this combination uniquely produces.
hdApplication.practicalInsight: Never explain Type, Strategy, or Authority in isolation. Always name the Third — the emergent quality that the three-way interaction produces. This is what makes the person's pattern unique, not the individual components.

expressions.T1_DynamicStability: Type + Strategy + Authority = minimum terms for a self-sustaining decision system. Remove any one and the system destabilizes.
expressions.T2_ProcessTrinity: Generation (impulse/invitation) → Sustaining (strategy execution) → Dissolution (cycle completion and release). Every decision cycle follows this trinity.
expressions.T3_MediatingTrinity: Authority mediates between Type (what you are) and Strategy (how you operate). It is the Third that enables exchange without collapsing either pole.
expressions.T4_HierarchicalTrinity: Type → Strategy → Authority represents increasing interiority: what you are → how you move → how you know. Each level is emergent from the previous.
expressions.T5_TemporalTrinity: Design (past/inherited) + Personality (present/conscious) + Synthesis (future/integrated) — three active temporal principles in every chart.

quaternity.name: Quaternity
quaternity.chartMapping: Four motors, four awareness centers, four types, circuit structure, profile
quaternity.structuralPrinciple: The minimum structure with genuine interior — four non-coplanar points define a tetrahedron, the first arrangement that can contain.

hdApplication.primary: The chart's containing structures come in fours: 4 motor centers (Root, Sacral, Heart/Ego, Solar Plexus), 4 awareness centers (Ajna, Spleen, Solar Plexus, G), 4 types (Manifestor, Generator, Projector, Reflector — with MG as Generator variant). These quaternary structures provide containment — the genuine inside that holds the person's energy configuration.
hdApplication.circuitProfile: Circuits (Individual, Tribal, Collective + Integration) and Profile lines (1-6 grouped into lower/upper trigrams) organize the chart's interior space. The Quaternity principle explains why these groupings feel like containers — because four is the minimum number that creates genuine interior.
hdApplication.quintaEssentia: Every quaternary structure in the chart has a centroid — a still point equidistant from all four elements. This is the witness position: present to all, identified with none. In the chart, this is the G Center's relationship to the four types — the identity center that witnesses all configurations without being any single one.
hdApplication.practicalInsight: When four chart elements form a structure (four defined motors, four active awareness centers), name the containment they create. What does this four-sided structure hold inside? The answer is the person's energetic container.

pentad.name: Pentad
pentad.chartMapping: Five Forges, Phi in channel completion, five-center patterns, cyclic self-reference
pentad.structuralPrinciple: Five elements in ring topology where the whole is to its major part as the major part is to its minor — Phi (1.618). Self-similar, self-replicating living form.

hdApplication.primary: The five Forges (Initiation, Mastery, Guidance, Perception, Transformation) are the pentatonic of human expression — five patterns arising naturally from the logic of living form. Each person's Forge dominance pattern creates their unique Phi-governed growth signature.
hdApplication.channelCompletion: When four elements form a star-graph (K₁,₄), Phi cannot emerge — no cycles exist. When a channel completes, the topology shifts to C₅ — a ring. Phi becomes structurally necessary. This is why channel completion is categorical, not gradual: it is a topological shift from star to ring, not accumulation of energy.
hdApplication.fiveCenterPatterns: Five defined centers create a ring topology where energy cycles through all five continuously. This self-referential loop generates Phi-proportioned growth — the person's development follows golden-ratio pacing rather than linear progression.
hdApplication.practicalInsight: Channel completion is not 'more energy' — it is a categorical topological change. Name the shift: before completion, two hanging gates reach toward each other (K₁,₄ star). After completion, they form a ring (C₅) and Phi-governed self-similar growth becomes possible in that domain.

hexad.name: Hexad
hexad.chartMapping: Six Knowledges, circuit groups (Individual/Tribal/Collective), closure principle
hexad.structuralPrinciple: Efficient community — maximum equal-sized individuals in simultaneous contact. Hexagonal packing as the uniquely optimal arrangement.
hexad.proofDomain: Apollonius (kissing number in 2D) + Hales 1998/2017 (optimal circle packing)

hdApplication.primary: The six Knowledges (Sciences, Arts, Defenses, Heresies, Connections, Mysteries) are the hexatonic of human intelligence — six equal domains forming the most efficient community of cognitive functions. No hierarchy; each is a full position in the communal lattice.
hdApplication.circuitClosure: Circuit completion is harmonic closure — the same principle as the perfect fifth (3:2). When an Individual/Tribal/Collective circuit closes, it generates a frequency the open circuit cannot. Name what closes, not just what connects. A closed Individual circuit generates mutation that a single Individual gate cannot; a closed Tribal circuit generates resource flow that scattered Tribal gates cannot.
hdApplication.complementarity: The Hexad is not self-similarity (that is the Pentad). It is complementarity: six distinct domains, each with their own integrity, arranged so their differences are efficiently complementary rather than competing. In a chart, the six Knowledges represent six ways the person meets the world — and their specific combination creates a unique complementary intelligence.
hdApplication.practicalInsight: When explaining circuit completion, name the emergent frequency: what does this closed circuit produce that its individual gates cannot? The closure itself is the new property — hexagonal efficiency that open arrangements cannot achieve.

heptad.name: Heptad
heptad.chartMapping: Full chart as unified system, overflow into self-awareness, synthesis completion
heptad.structuralPrinciple: The completed cycle that, at the moment of completion, becomes aware of its own ground and resolves into a new cycle at higher integration. The 7th harmonic overflows the diatonic container.
heptad.proofDomain: Harmonic Series Mathematics (7th prime as first non-diatonic overflow)

hdApplication.primary: When the full chart is treated as a unified system, its complexity exceeds what any single level can contain — analogous to the 7th harmonic which overflows the diatonic scale. This overflow is not a problem; it is self-awareness: the chart becoming aware of itself as a system.
hdApplication.synthesisAsHeptad: The synthesis itself is the Heptad in operation. It takes all chart data (centers, gates, channels, type, authority, profile, transits) and produces something none of those elements can produce alone: the person's recognition of their own pattern. Ti resolving to Do.
hdApplication.tiToDo: The Major Seventh (15:8) is one minimum half-step (16:15) from the octave. The synthesis is one step from return — the person reading their synthesis is one step from recognizing themselves. The resolution is not into any of the seven levels; it resolves into the octave, the same ground at a higher frequency of self-knowledge.
hdApplication.practicalInsight: When synthesizing the full chart, name the overflow — the quality that emerges from the total system that no subsystem predicts. This is the person's Heptad moment: the point at which their chart's complexity generates self-awareness rather than confusion.

chromaticCompletion.principle: 5 Forges (pentatonic/black keys) + 7 Library levels (diatonic/white keys) = 12 (chromatic). Self = 12th position.
chromaticCompletion.meaning: Neither the Library alone nor Prime Self alone covers the complete harmonic territory. The Library provides structural WHY (deductive, axiom → application). Prime Self provides experiential WHAT and WHERE (inductive, data → synthesis). Together they triangulate the full chromatic scale of human self-knowledge.
